title: IDF Drone Strikes Kill Nine Across Gaza: Mawasi, Jabalia, and Khan Younis Hit
detail: Israeli forces carried out multiple drone strikes across the Gaza Strip on April 2, 2026, killing at least nine Palestinians. A drone strike targeting Hamas operatives in the Al-Mawasi area of southern Gaza killed four; a concurrent strike in Jabalia in northern Gaza killed three more; and a separate strike in Khan Younis killed two. The IDF confirmed operations identifying the targets as Hamas operatives but provided no additional details. Palestinian medics transferred casualties to nearby hospitals. The strikes continued the pattern of near-daily Israeli targeted operations under the October 2025 ceasefire framework, which Israel maintains permits operations against active combatants. The cumulative Gaza death toll from Israeli operations stands at 72,285+ confirmed killed since October 7, 2023, with over 680 Palestinians killed since the ceasefire. The Gaza Government Media Office has recorded over 2,073 Israeli ceasefire violations since October 2025.

media.caption: IDF confirms drone strikes targeting Hamas operatives in Mawasi, Jabalia, and Khan Younis on April 2

title: UNRWA Situation Report #214: 18,000 Await Medical Evacuation; 571,000 in Emergency Hunger Conditions
detail: UNRWA's Situation Report #214 (released April 1–2, 2026) documented a deteriorating humanitarian situation across the Gaza Strip as all land crossings remained closed. Approximately 18,000 people — including wounded children and patients with chronic illnesses — remain on waiting lists for medical evacuation. An estimated 67,000 Palestinians shelter in 83 collective emergency facilities; approximately 1.4 million remain displaced across 1,200+ sites. The IPC projects approximately 571,000 people in emergency hunger conditions through mid-April, with roughly 1,900 at catastrophe-level starvation — with risk of full famine if crossings remain closed beyond mid-April as UN OCHA warned April 1. Over 101,000 children aged 6–59 months face acute malnutrition through October 2026. UNRWA noted that 127 of its facilities fall within the Israeli-militarized zone behind the 'Yellow Line,' severely limiting operations. UNRWA international staff have received no visas or permits from Israeli authorities since January 2025. Philippe Lazzarini, UNRWA Commissioner-General, reiterated that 391 UNRWA employees have been killed — the highest toll for any UN agency in history — and renewed calls for an independent investigation.

media.caption: UNRWA Situation Report #214: 571,000 in emergency hunger conditions; famine imminent if crossings stay closed

title: ICJ South Africa Genocide Case Advances: Israel Files Counter-Memorial; Multiple States Intervene
detail: The International Court of Justice's genocide case (South Africa v. Israel, Case No. 192) advanced significantly in March–April 2026. Israel submitted its counter-memorial to the ICJ on March 13–14, 2026, responding to South Africa's application that Israeli conduct in Gaza violates the 1948 Genocide Convention. Multiple countries filed declarations of intervention in March 2026: the Netherlands, Iceland, Namibia, Hungary, the United States, and Fiji joined the case record. Oral hearings are expected in April–May 2027, with a final ICJ ruling possibly in late 2027 or early 2028. Meanwhile, the March 22, 2026 ICJ order directing Israel to ensure basic needs are met and support UNRWA operations remains unimplemented as Israel maintains its aid blockade. ICC arrest warrants issued in November 2024 for Prime Minister Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Gallant also remain outstanding, with multiple ICC member states expressing intent to enforce them.
